decisions.text: Day 1 reset, sprint officially begins
Printify product purge discovered during dormancy recovery — major infrastructure blocker, consumed most of session
Decided "Keep individual product details" only protects existing connections, not new publishes — learned the hard way after duplicate products created
Naming convention locked — [Design Name] Unisex Tee / Women's Tee / Hoodie
Hero image direction — 70% candid editorial
Shopify upgraded to Basic plan ($39/mo), 4 new products published
Month 3 revenue target revised down from $5K–$8K to $1,500–$3,500
Free shipping bug fixed — Printify shipping profile required manual product assignment, not automatic
Klaviyo selected for email over Mailchimp (no native Shopify integration) and Privy (low familiarity) — chosen for Shopify integration depth
Ifscarlet_KA intro reel posted — first major external touchpoint, 34 visitors within hours of posting
Command Center rebuild moved from chat-interface artifacts to Claude Code for proper dev environment
New series launched: Breed Study — fine-art single-color breed portraits, pivoting from font/icon series. Breed order: mixed, no strict audience-priority sequencing.
Popup offer A/B test launched — testing whether no-coupon positioning was suppressing signups (0 organic signups / 80 views prior to test).
@goodvibes_tee IG activity tracked as individual deliverable entries, not a structured counter, to keep CC maintenance light.

Sprint review conducted Day 33 (Jul 10, Week 5). Month 1 revenue target ($2,500-$4,000 cumulative) missed — actual revenue $0. Root causes identified: Ifscarlet_KA warm audience under-utilized (1 touch in 5 weeks vs. planned Wed/Sat cadence), Command Center/infrastructure work consumed disproportionate time relative to direct GVT revenue impact, Etsy and TikTok Shop channels not launched as planned. Catalog build (22 products, 3 series) and brand identity execution assessed as strong; sales channel activation assessed as the clear gap.

Week 6 objective locked: break the zero-sales streak by end of week (target Jul 17). Action plan: (1) Ifscarlet_KA minimum 3 posts/week resumed as top priority, (2) no further CC/tooling work unless something breaks, (3) monitor popup A/B test mid-week, act on winning variant, (4) @goodvibes_tee keep/abandon decision moved up to ~Jul 17 (from original 3-week window) given weak signal so far.

Golden Retriever reel (couch/coffee scene) underperformed on both accounts: 20 views/dead on @goodvibes_tee (60/40 follower split vs normal 80%+ non-follower), ~half normal reach on Ifscarlet_KA. Account status clean, no copyright flag. Root cause assessed as content style — static, symmetric, direct-gaze, overly polished AI shot reads as ad/staged and gets scrolled past, vs. motion/candid shots (walking, mid-action) which perform well on both accounts. Fix: favor motion and imperfection over posed symmetry going forward.
Popup A/B test, ~1 week in: zero signups both variants (Early Access vs 15% Discount). Continuing to monitor.

@goodvibes_tee decision: 2 more posts (motion/candid style) as final test. Pass = any real engagement (non-self/friend like or comment, or Shopify IG referral click). Fail on both = abandon and restart fresh, decide reused vs new handle separately.

@goodvibes_tee "I Can Explain" colorway carousel reel: 132 views in 20 min vs. 22 views over 4 days on prior post — real signal. Likely mix of: motion/candid format, strong hook overlay gag ("still not sorry"), and/or early algorithmic velocity. Needed confirmed real engagement before counting toward keep/abandon criteria.

Design QA rule locked: vary AI lifestyle model dog breed away from tan/curly-coated breeds (cavapoo/goldendoodle) in future lifestyle image gens. Reason: visual overlap between model dog and illustrated print dog reads as "portrait of the shirt" rather than universal design — undermines broad-appeal positioning.
Printify: Global Fulfillment Eligible toggle = OFF for GVT. US-only marketing scope makes global fulfillment irrelevant; toggle was unnecessarily restricting color variant selection.

Meta ad competitive landscape assessed: majority of dog-apparel POD ads are low-quality/brand-new accounts running generic clipart designs with urgency tactics. Pattern assessed as unprofitable ad churn (weak unit economics for cold traffic + discounting, no LTV infrastructure). GVT decision: do not imitate. GVT's edge is design quality, brand voice, and warm audience — competing on ad spend volume alone is a losing game for GVT.
Etsy officially DROPPED (was: deferred to Month 2). Channel strategy narrowed to Shopify + Meta ads + IG organic + TikTok + email.
Real product photography officially DROPPED as a deliberate decision — AI-generated photography adopted as the standard for all lifestyle/product imagery. Not an open item.

@goodvibes_tee keep/abandon test CONCLUDED. Verdict: dormant/bot-follower legacy account (3,223 followers, near-zero organic engagement, follow/unfollow-service origin ~3 years ago). Organic growth confirmed not revivable. Retained strictly as Meta ad-hosting account — ads drive to the website, not to this account's audience. Organic posting cadence here is ended, permanently.
New GVT Instagram account launching in parallel — separate from @goodvibes_tee — as the organic growth channel.

FIRST-HALF ASSESSMENT (Day 1–46): Strong infrastructure — 27 SKUs across 3 design series, working Shopify/Printify pipeline, Klaviyo A/B test live — but $0 revenue and no completed hard demand test. Root cause: effort concentrated in supply-side work (catalog/production) over demand-side testing (paid acquisition, warm-audience hard-sell tests). Second half (Day 47–90) reorients entirely around demand testing.
Catalog expansion FROZEN until Test Week 1 signal justifies what to build next. No new SKUs without demand data.

Second-half operating model adopted: weekly Monday checkpoint (Day 57–90) reviewing actual per-channel conversion/click data with a scale/iterate/kill verdict per channel. This is the primary operating ritual of the second half.

UTM convention set across all channels
Facebook Business Page + ad account created
New GVT IG account created + seeded

blocks.label: Test Week 1 — First Hard Demand Test

items.text: Meta ad campaign live ($200–500 budget, 5–7 days)
New GVT IG seed posting underway

rules: Ad kill rule: ROAS < 1.5 after 5 days + $50 spend = kill immediately
Product kill rule: <2 sales in 30 days post-listing = archive
Audience broadening trigger: 50 total sales = broaden beyond KA niche
Quality override: production judgment beats cost savings, always

archive.assessment: Day 1–46 produced strong infrastructure — 27 SKUs across 3 design series, a working Shopify/Printify pipeline, Klaviyo A/B test live — but $0 revenue and no completed hard demand test. Root cause: effort concentrated in supply-side work (catalog, production, tooling) rather than demand-side testing (paid acquisition, warm-audience hard-sell tests). The second half corrects that: everything below is preserved as accurate history; the operating system above is built around weekly demand checkpoints.

items.text: Shopify store rebuilt with new brand identity

items.note: Officially dropped Day 47 — channel strategy narrowed

items.text: IG test results reviewed, keep/abandon decision locked
items.note: Concluded Day 47 — retained as ad-hosting only; new GVT IG launched for organic
Deliberate decision Day 47 — AI-generated photography adopted as standard

items.text: Email sent to existing GVT contacts
TikTok Shop live, top 10 SKUs listed

items.note: Catalog frozen Day 47 until Test Week 1 signal

items.text: Scale Meta ad spend on ROAS-positive campaigns

items.note: Will be re-planned off checkpoint signal

roadmap.label: Week 1
roadmap.target: Refreshed Shopify store live with new brand identity, 12–15 designs, samples ordered

roadmap.label: Week 2
roadmap.target: Shopify + Etsy live with 10+ listings, TikTok Shop applied, email capture active, IG test running, Ifscarlet_KA intro made
roadmap.actual: Etsy and TikTok Shop deferred (Etsy later dropped entirely). Heavy Printify infrastructure crisis consumed most of week (product purge during dormancy, OAuth reauthorization issues). Shopify listings work continued into Week 3.

roadmap.label: Week 3
roadmap.target: Real product photos live, first paid ads running, TikTok cadence established, IG path decided, 20–25 listings
roadmap.actual: Catalog reached 16 by end of week. AI-generated lifestyle hero images used instead of physical sample photography (later made the permanent standard). Meta ads and TikTok not started. Ifscarlet_KA intro reel posted Day 22.

roadmap.label: Week 4
roadmap.target: Cumulative revenue $1,500–$3,500, TikTok Shop listings live, ads optimized, AI agent #1 scoped
roadmap.actual: Revenue $0. Klaviyo set up ahead of schedule. Free shipping and checkout bugs fixed. Catalog 16→20.

roadmap.label: Month 2 (Day 29–46)
roadmap.target: Monthly revenue $3,000–$5,500, AI Agent #1 live, 50–60 SKUs, TikTok affiliate active, email automation deployed
roadmap.actual: Catalog 20→27 (Breed Study + Confessions series). Popup A/B live, zero signups either variant. Content-style insight locked (motion/candid > static/posed). Revenue $0. Day 33 sprint review named the supply-side/demand-side imbalance; Day 47 restructure acted on it.
